title: coridochloa semialata
intro: Coridochloa semialata, also known as New Zealand button grass, is a type of plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its small, button-like flowers and slender leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Coridochloa semialata include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be used fresh or dried, and is often made into teas, tinctures, or poultices for topical application.
